rulings.Case Title: LARS VINKE VS. FRANCIS PECORARO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JULY 12, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT AQUA DEVELOPMENT CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT AQUA'S DUTY TO OPERATE A VALET SERVICE CAUSED THE ACCIDENT. THE UNDISPUTED FACTS ESTABLISH THAT IT WAS NOT THE MANNER IN WHICH THE VALET SERVICE OPERATED THAT CAUSED THE ACCIDENT, IT WAS PECORARO'S DECISION, MADE WITH FULL KNOWLEDGE OF THE FACTS, THAT CAUSED THE ACCIDENT. THE OBJECTION TO AYRES' DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED. ASSUMING ARGUENDO, HE IS EVEN QUALIFIED, THE SUBJECT OF THE DECLARATION IS NOT ONE FOR EXPERT TESTIMONY. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MOLLY PETTIT VS. ANITA MARGRILL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: And Notice Of Motion To Strike Df'S Untimely Anti-Slapp Motion And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JULY 12, 2007, LINE 13. PLAINTIFF MOLLY PETTIT'S Motion To Strike DEFENDANT'S Untimely Anti-SLAPP Motion And Request For Sanctions: THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED. THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION IS UNTIMELY AND DEFENDANT'S RATIONAL FOR THE DELAY IS UNTENABLE. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED (C.C.P. SECTION 425.16(C)) BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE MOTION WAS FILED FOR DELAY OR, IN FACT, HAS CAUSED ANY DELAY GIVEN THE ON-GOING DISCOVERY, AND THE COURT HAS NOT ADDRESSED THE MERITS OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION SO AS TO BE ABLE TO ADDRESS WHETHER THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION WAS "FRIVOLOUS". =(302/PJM/CB-X)

rulings.Case Title: MARY K POWELL VS. PATRICIA A DEAVER et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO PLACER COUNTY*****

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JULY 12, 2007, LINE 19. DEFENDANT PATRICIA DEAVER'S MOTION To Transfer (And REQUEST For Judicial NOTICE) IS GRANTED. REQUEST FOR AN AWARD OF FEES AND COSTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,805.00 IS GRANTED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: BLUE'S ROOFING COMPANY VS. THE WORKERS COMPENSATION INSURANCE RATING BUREAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Petitioner'S Second Amended Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate,
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JULY 12, 2007, LINE 20. PETITIONER BLUE'S ROOFING COMPANY'S Motion In Support Of Petitioner'S Second Amended Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate: The writ of mandate is denied requesting reversal of the Department of Insurance's decision of July 22, 2004. Although Petitioner does not address the standard of review, Petitioner has failed to carry its burden whether the standard be arbitrary or capricious or an abuse of discretion or substantial deference. Petitioner does not dispute the applicability of Rule 3(f) but it seeks equitable relief based on Star Roofing and/or the use of Petitioner's data. Star Roofing is limited to pre December 31, 2003 experience modification data. Rule 3(f), applicable to post January 1, 2004 data determinations, was adopted after public hearings. It reflects a balance between the need for data for an equitable statewide experience rating system and the individual employer's need for an experience rating that reflects its own experience. The rule is particularly applicable where, as here, the insolvent carrier has provided little or no data. Although the WCIRB is authorized to "verify any and all data", it is not authorized to ignore Rule 3(f) to exclude data of an insolvent carrier. Regarding the use of Petitioner's data, the very case upon which it relies, Star Roofing, rejects such data as "not sufficiently reliable." =(302/PJM/AP)
